title: Omiki & Vegas - Wana
visual_analysis: The reference piece behind motion.py's morph doctrine. Single ornate hero — a Mesoamerican warrior in a gold-and-red feathered headdress, turquoise inlay and tribal face paint, shot dead-center against a bioluminescent jungle-temple at dusk. The storyboard reveals the 7-minute flow: establishing shot of a stone eagle atop a carved temple face, pull-back to reveal a stepped pyramid complex at moonlit dusk, morph into a warrior-portrait hero, then into a golden sun-mask variant, with jungle pathways and ritual altars as connective tissue. Palette is cinematic teal-jungle + red-feather + gold-ornament on ink-black negative space. Subject is ALWAYS human/architectural, never abstract. Virality driver: a single recognizable Mesoamerican archetype the YouTube algorithm can thumbnail-preview from any frame, rendered with film-grade depth of field and practical dust-particle lighting.

title: Omiki x Sighter - Sofia (Extended Official Video)
visual_analysis: Near-future dystopian-ritual, also Omiki-brand but tonally colder than Wana. Ranks of gold-chrome-masked female figures in floral slip-dresses stand in serried rows under a silver moon — the mask is a tear-drop kabuki form with mirror-polished reflections that catch the light like chrome warriors. Storyboard shows a deep arc: full-moon crowd shot, monumental pyramid altar, chrome-masked close-up, a fiery passenger-ship crashing through a dust cloud, ritual embrace between two figures, tattoo-covered hand-clasp, then back to the gold-masked legion marching at dawn. Palette: desaturated ivory skin + gold chrome + night-indigo + floral accent. The viral hook is the army-of-identical-masks pattern — it reads as sacred-ceremony + sci-fi at once, and the repeated-subject composition makes every frame a viable thumbnail.

title: Charlotte de Witte - High Street (Astrix remix)
visual_analysis: The most-viewed psytrance visualizer in this corpus (11M+). Pure abstract sacred-geometry — a fractal Egyptian-pharaoh head dissolves into kaleidoscopic temple architecture, faceted lapis-blue and ochre-gold, eyes glowing amber. The storyboard pivots between fractal cityscapes (skyscraper-tunnel zooms), mandala floor rooms, and radial-symmetry halls seen through a fisheye; almost no human subjects, no narrative. This is the classic Astrix channel formula: repeating symmetry at every scale, zoom that never ends, algorithmic-looking ornament. Palette: electric cyan + burnt orange + pharaoh-gold on coal black. The viral mechanic is hypnotic repetition — viewers report losing track of time, and the algorithm surfaces it because session time per viewer is enormous.

title: Astrix - Coolio (Darwish Remix) [8K]
visual_analysis: Full-saturation psychedelic pop-surrealism — a third-eye-pierced android head caked in polychrome pimple-textures, flanked by solar-flare starbursts, dreadlock columns of micro-dots, spinning vinyl/UFO disks. The storyboard is a mandala catalogue: every frame is a radial starburst with a different hero in the center (an alien totem, a skull-mask, a rocket, a robot face). Palette: neon rainbow on black — yellow / magenta / cyan / lime in equal aggressive mix. This is the ornament-maximalist pole of psytrance: no restraint, no palette limit, kinetic texture over clean composition. Virality: the 8K label + the sheer texture density make it feel like premium content, and every thumbnail clickthrough delivers on the 'overwhelming' promise.

title: Moonclipse - Dunya (Terra & Omiki Remix) [8K]
visual_analysis: Shamanic-jungle feminine hero with bioluminescent Amazonian face paint, flower crown, orange glowing irises and a river of liquid fire spilling from her throat — lit in dusk-teal jungle with orange ember accents. Storyboard reveals a Shamanic-Tales-branded compendium: twinned eagle/parrot spirit-beast bookends, mandala forest circles, tribal silhouettes gathered around a sunburst mandala, stone idol pairs, and communal fire-ceremony scenes around a sunburst talisman. Palette: jungle teal + orange-fire + gold ornament, very close to Holy Rave's existing fire/warrior vocabulary. The subject is explicitly sacred-ceremony rather than ornament — this is the closest psytrance cousin to what System B should be doing.

title: Astrix & Ritmo - Ziran (Antinomy Remix)
visual_analysis: Stained-glass cathedral eye-of-Providence — an enormous single eye with a peacock-feather aureole, staring from a fractured-glass rose-window facade. Storyboard walks through an Antinomy 'Shamanic Tales' sister look: stained-glass desert landscapes with moon-arch doorways, nested eye mandalas, geometric peacock fans, minor-key pastel greens and lilacs. Palette shifts away from Astrix-neon toward muted sage + rose + dusty gold — almost Art Nouveau. Subject is the repeating eye motif (third-eye, all-seeing, peacock) used as a meditative hypnotic anchor. Virality: the calmer palette reads as 'premium / tasteful' in a genre dominated by neon overload; it positions as 'psytrance for adults'.

title: Ace Ventura & GMS - Mindshift
visual_analysis: Critical outlier — the storyboard is LIVE NATURE FOOTAGE: drone pass over a foggy dawn meadow dotted with trees, a mossy forest stream with sun-backlit mist, a paisley-printed festival canopy at sunset, macro shots of lichen-flecked bark, bokeh lens-flare fire-pit. The thumbnail is a full-body UV-painted female figure in a mandala prayer-pose against deep-violet + neon-peacock. So this is a *two-register* visualizer: the clickbait thumbnail is maximalist tribal hero, but the actual video body is slow-cinema forest-Goa footage. Palette: dawn-grey + moss-green + sunset-pink for the body, neon violet + cyan for the cover. Virality: the thumbnail does the CTR work and the calm nature footage retains psy-lifestyle viewers for the full session.

title: Ace Ventura & StarLab - Maana
visual_analysis: Indigenous/Amazonian masked priestess in red-and-black feather crown, jungle warpaint, torch-bearing dancers behind her — pure rainforest-ritual maximalism. Storyboard shows a strongly architectural arc: crowds gathering before a rainbow waterfall doorway, approaching a glowing stone temple, fire-filled altar shot between carved pillars, flame-wrapped warrior rising from the fire, then a meditating figure in lotus inside a torus of glyphs, with butterflies and tropical birds swirling. Palette: torch-orange + forest-green + obsidian black with red accents. This is temple-ritual-as-animation cinema; the viral formula is 'one hero on thumbnail, epic ceremonial arc inside'. It's the strongest blueprint in the corpus for a RJM-native System B — swap Amazon ritual for Hebrew altar ritual and the compositions transfer almost verbatim.

title: Omiki - Todo (Official Video)
visual_analysis: Minimal-loop single-asset visualizer — a porcelain-faced 3D bust, half-flowered purple-mauve bouquet growing from the skull, floats centered in a receding wireframe box-field against matte black with yellow sans-serif 'TODO' above. The storyboard grid shows the same composition 9 times with small rotations — this is a 10-second loop stretched across 7 minutes, NOT a morph chain. Palette: black + violet + bone-white + acid-yellow text. Teaches an important contrast lesson: Omiki does not always do the Wana-style cinematic chain — for smaller releases he ships a single hero loop, because the algorithm/CTR still works off a strong static frame. Virality: premium 'cover-art-in-motion' aesthetic at near-zero production cost.

title: Naeleck x Vini Vici - Home Alone (with Marnik) (Extended Mix)
visual_analysis: Pure static cover-art visualizer — crimson-black scratchy texture field, a silhouetted figure framed in a bright magenta doorway-portal of light, horror-movie display serif for 'HOME ALONE' up top. The storyboard grid is 9 nearly-identical tiles — zero on-screen motion beyond subtle grain. This is the floor of the psytrance-visualizer spectrum: a $0 still with title-card typography, published by a headline act, still clears 1.6M views because the track and channel do the work. Palette: deep oxblood + magenta + bone. Teaches that for a name act, the visual can be minimal if the thumbnail is a strong single image. Not a template for Holy Rave but a useful lower-bound reference.

title: Adam Port, Stryv - Move feat. Malachiii (Extended Version)
visual_analysis: The most-viewed track in the entire corpus (102M+) and the archetype of the Keinemusik visualizer look: a hand-painted crimson field divided down the middle, an orange vinyl record ghosted through, and the word MOVE scrawled in blackletter-graffiti across the seam. Artist credits in rough stencil bottom-left, Keinemusik logo bottom-right. The storyboard is 30 near-identical tiles — the entire 6-minute track is essentially a static album cover with a barely-perceptible slow pan. This is radical minimalism as aesthetic statement: the track is the content, the art is a flag. Palette: single dominant vermilion, one off-red accent, black ink. Viral driver is the Keinemusik brand stamp — fans recognize the hand-font across every release and click on reputation.

title: Keinemusik (Rampa, &ME, Adam Port) - Say What (feat. Chuala)
visual_analysis: Crushed-VHS documentary still — three Keinemusik members lounging on a hotel bed in white tees and sunglasses, caught in a deliberately ugly webcam-era grab, with SAY WHAT FEAT CHUALA chrome-bubble text pasted diagonally across the frame. Storyboard: nine identical tiles. The aesthetic choice is anti-design — deliberately tacky, deliberately snapshot-grade, because the Keinemusik posture is 'we are the product, the music is the product'. Palette: washed-out flesh + beige walls + chrome text. Lesson: at maximum brand-equity, a crew photo beats a cinematic render. Holy Rave cannot borrow this directly (brand equity not there yet) but it defines the ceiling of 'minimalist wins because the act is the icon'.

title: &ME, Black Coffee - The Rapture Pt.III
visual_analysis: Liquid-flesh fluid-paint abstract — swirling pink/coral/ochre marbling with holes burning through like cigarette-paper, neon-lime hand-lettered track title wobbling across. Storyboard is 25 tiles of the same fluid-paint texture slowly recirculating — it's a single generative loop, likely a pigment-in-water shader or real Ebru marbling footage running on a loop. Palette: flesh-pink + ochre + lime-text on off-white. This is the 'abstract-organic' pole of the Keinemusik look — no faces, no scene, just matter. Viral mechanic is the genre marker: organic-house fans associate marbled-pigment aesthetics with quality, same way psy fans associate fractals with quality.

title: Keinemusik (Adam Port, &ME, Rampa), Alan Dixon - Thandaza feat. Arabic Piano
visual_analysis: Airbrushed-poster aesthetic — ultramarine sky, a dove-like bird frozen mid-wing, pink-and-chrome paint drips across the lower third, tiny dancing figures silhouetted at the base, bold white sans-serif 'THANDAZA' smeared across the middle. Storyboard: nine near-identical tiles — again a static cover with micro-motion. Palette: deep cobalt + shell-pink + bone-white. The composition is flat graphic-design, not cinematic 3D — more poster than photograph. Viral driver again is Keinemusik brand consistency: the 'painted-poster + hand-type' signature is the Keinemusik visual genre at this point.

title: Ben Böhmer - Rust (Official Visualiser)
visual_analysis: Anamorphic macro of a wedding-style floral bouquet — pale peonies, yellow mimosa sprays, eucalyptus leaves, shot with prismatic rainbow flare across the left edge. Storyboard shows the 4:30 video is ONE continuous slow macro push through the same bouquet, slightly rotating; almost no scene change. Palette: bone-white + mustard-yellow + sage-green + teal-denim accents. This is the Anjunadeep-flavoured 'tasteful and emotional' organic aesthetic — no body, no face, just beautiful material. Virality: acts as a 4-minute emotional contemplative surface; pairs to Böhmer's ambient-progressive sound like wallpaper to chamber music.

title: Stryv, Malachiii, Adam Port - Positions
visual_analysis: Raw intimate lo-fi VHS — blurred close-up of a woman in white lace lingerie (belly/collarbone region), with the word POSITIONS hand-drawn graffiti across her chest, lyrics ('it could feel good if you want', 'make ya tailbone jump') flickering in mid-frame captions. Storyboard is a 9-tile catalogue of warm-beige body fragments, red-tinted hips, a denim-skirt jump, with lyric overlays — it's a lyric video masquerading as body-art cinema. Palette: amber-flesh + crimson + ivory. This is Keinemusik's sexiest-brand output and the most 'moving' of their visualizers (there is actual cut-to-beat editing). Virality: the lingerie frame is thumbnail-magnetic, and the rotating-body fragments + caption pulses give viewers something to stay on beat with.

title: Keinemusik, Boys Noize - Crazy For It (feat. Vinson)
visual_analysis: Horror-woodcut aesthetic — a ghostly white silhouette of a figure crouched in a misty forest-glen, the title CRAZY FOR IT dripping in blood-red brush-graffiti across the top, tree-branches clawing in from the edges. Storyboard: 9 nearly-identical tiles, barely-perceptible pan. Palette: monochrome charcoal + bone-white + blood-red. This is the darkest Keinemusik cover — closer to a metal-band single-art than organic-house. Virality: extreme palette restraint + horror-adjacent typography cuts through the pastel-organic feed and earns clicks on sheer tonal contrast.

title: Ben Böhmer - One Last Call ft. Felix Raphael (Official Visualiser)
visual_analysis: Liquid-metal oil-on-water macro — lavender, cobalt, and marigold pigments swirling across a dark substrate, frosted-glass refraction in the upper third, serif title card reading 'Ben Böhmer — One Last Call'. Storyboard is 9 tiles of the same marbled slick, slowly re-patterning. Palette: lilac + cobalt + saffron + graphite. This is the canonical Anjunadeep-side organic-house look: refined, flat-lay-magazine, painterly but abstract. No human subject. Virality: pairs beautifully with background-listening sessions — Anjunadeep's dominant use case is 'on in the background while working', and a hypnotic marble surface is perfect for that.

title: Keinemusik, Sevdaliza - See You Again
visual_analysis: Post-punk zine collage — cut-out torn-paper portrait fragments of the four artists pasted against a fire-engine-red field, each pair of eyes redacted with a solid red bar in censored-document style. Bottom-left white sans 'SEE YOU AGAIN', top-left 'KEINEMUSIK', top-right 'SEVDALIZA'. Palette: one-color red + newsprint-gray + black bars. This is the label's most Situationist-flavoured cover — punk fanzine + redacted-document as organic-house visual identity. It's almost anti-visualizer; the statement is 'we're not showing you anything beautiful'. Virality: inversion works because every other release in viewer's feed is tasteful marbling/desert-photo — the red blare stops scroll.

title: &ME - L.I.F.E
visual_analysis: Companion piece to 'Rapture Pt.III' — the same fluid-paint marbling, this time a rose-peach base with deeper burgundy wells and the yellow-graffiti '&ME / L.I.F.E / keinemusik' signature typography. Probably the same underlying generator, slightly retinted. Palette: rose + burgundy + acid-yellow + black voids. Static 7-minute loop, no cut, no scene. This proves the Keinemusik visualizer is a FORMULA — pigment + hand-letters + one dominant warm — not a bespoke art piece per track. The scalability lesson is important: one template served across dozens of releases keeps the brand visually coherent and production cost near zero.
